reasons: Khufra directly punishes Fanny's main strength because Bouncing Ball can stop enemies trying to blink across him.
Fanny depends on clean Steel Cable paths to enter and escape, while Khufra can block those paths near walls, jungle entrances, and objective fights.

proof.summary: Fanny wants uninterrupted cable movement through terrain, while Khufra can punish blink-style movement with Bouncing Ball and stop her from freely crossing his zone.
worksBestWhen: Khufra positions near Fanny's expected wall path.
Khufra saves Bouncing Ball for Fanny's real cable entry.
Allies immediately follow up when Fanny is interrupted.
failureCases: Fanny avoids Khufra's zone and attacks from another wall angle.
Khufra activates Bouncing Ball too early.
Khufra stops Fanny but has no team follow-up damage.

reasons: Franco can punish Fanny with Iron Hook displacement if he predicts her cable route or waits near the target she wants to dive.
Bloody Hunt suppression can stop Fanny during her commit window and give Franco's team time to burst her down.

proof.summary: Fanny wants to enter and exit quickly with Steel Cable, while Franco can interrupt her commit with hook displacement or suppression if he times the punish correctly.
worksBestWhen: Franco waits near the ally Fanny wants to dive.
Franco uses Bloody Hunt after Fanny commits, not before she chooses her route.
The team is ready to burst Fanny during the suppression window.
failureCases: Fanny attacks from an unexpected wall angle.
Franco suppresses Fanny without enough follow-up damage.

reasons: Kaja's Divine Judgement can suppress and drag Fanny, stopping her cable sequence after she commits.
Kaja is especially useful when he holds ultimate for Fanny instead of using it early on a lower-value target.

proof.summary: Fanny wants to pass through fights with cable mobility, while Kaja can suppress and drag her during the commit window, preventing her from freely continuing the cable sequence.
worksBestWhen: Kaja waits until Fanny enters his range.
Kaja has teammates nearby to damage Fanny during suppression.
Kaja saves Divine Judgement for Fanny's real dive.
failureCases: Fanny attacks the opposite side of the fight.
Kaja uses Divine Judgement on another hero before Fanny commits.
Kaja is zoned or bursted before Fanny enters.

reasons: Obsidia can punish Fanny with Hunt of Bone because the tether can prevent displacement skills during its active window.
This creates a strong anti-mobility window against Fanny, but Obsidia must land the ultimate and avoid being bursted first.

proof.summary: Fanny wants to keep flying through walls and reposition freely, while Obsidia's Hunt of Bone can tether her and prevent displacement skills during the tether window.
worksBestWhen: Obsidia casts Hunt of Bone after Fanny commits.
Obsidia has team follow-up ready during the tether duration.
The fight happens near a wall path or objective where Fanny's cable route is predictable.
failureCases: Fanny bursts Obsidia before the ultimate connects.
Fanny baits the ultimate, disengages, then re-enters after it ends.

reasons: Minsitthar can restrict Fanny's movement inside King's Calling, making her cable entry or escape much harder if she is caught inside the field.
This counter is valid but situational because Minsitthar must place the field where Fanny actually commits.

proof.summary: Fanny wants to cable through terrain and exit after burst damage, while Minsitthar can create a field that restricts movement skills if Fanny is caught inside it.
worksBestWhen: Minsitthar places King's Calling on Fanny's target or escape path.
Fanny has already committed before Minsitthar casts the field.
The team fights inside the field instead of chasing outside it.
failureCases: Minsitthar casts King's Calling too early.
Fanny waits until King's Calling is unavailable before committing.
